title: Zephyranthes parvula
taxonomy: Zephyranthes parvula belongs to the family Amaryllidaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the amaryllis family. It is a genus of flowering plants that are native to the tropical and subtropical regions of the Americas. Within this genus, Zephyranthes parvula is a species of flowering plant that is known for its small white blooms. The classification of Zephyranthes parvula is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Angiospermophyta, and class Monocotyledoneae. Its specific classification is a subject of ongoing research and study in the field of botany.

intro: Zephyranthes parvula, commonly known as the small rain lily, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the family Amaryllidaceae. The small rain lily has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, wounds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Zephyranthes parvula include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
